event_name: OPEC+ Production Cut — Energy Sector Stress Scenario
description: OPEC+ announces surprise coordinated production cut of 1.5M barrels/day effective immediately, driving Brent crude above $100/bbl. Downstream refiners face margin compression while upstream E&P companies experience mixed impact depending on hedging positions and leverage. Energy transition narratives accelerate pressure on debt-heavy oil companies.

direct_exposure.rationale: XOM is a fully integrated major with significant upstream exposure to crude pricing. A sustained price increase improves revenue but OPEC+ instability raises geopolitical risk and supply chain uncertainty for long-term project financing. Hedging coverage estimated at 40% for 2024.
direct_exposure.alert_message: Energy sector stress elevated — review covenant compliance and borrowing base.

direct_exposure.rationale: OXY carries higher leverage than peers and is more sensitive to oil price swings. While higher crude prices temporarily improve cash flows, the company's $18B debt load and Permian Basin concentration make it vulnerable if OPEC+ reverses course. Existing CRITICAL rating now confirmed by external stress signal.
direct_exposure.alert_message: CRITICAL — OXY leverage ratio under stress. Immediate covenant review recommended. Escalate to credit committee.

indirect_exposure.rationale: Boeing's aerospace manufacturing supply chain relies heavily on petrochemical inputs (composites, resins, lubricants). An OPEC+ shock elevates input costs and could delay airline customer orders if fuel cost pressure reduces airline capex. Boeing's existing WATCH status makes this an additive risk factor.
indirect_exposure.alert_message: Indirect supply chain exposure to energy shock — monitor quarterly order book and customer deferrals.

no_exposure.rationale: JPMorgan is a diversified financial institution. While higher oil prices could marginally affect loan portfolio quality in energy-adjacent industries, JPM's diversification, strong capital ratios (CET1 ~15%), and investment-grade rating insulate it from this specific commodity shock. No direct commodity exposure.

Amazon's primary revenue drivers (AWS cloud, e-commerce, advertising) are not materially correlated with crude oil prices. Logistics fuel costs represent a modest and manageable portion of COGS with significant hedging and fleet electrification underway. No meaningful risk uplift from this event.

Pfizer operates in pharmaceuticals where commodity energy prices have negligible impact on core business drivers (drug pipeline, patent lifecycle, regulatory approvals). Manufacturing energy costs are immaterial relative to R&D and SG&A. No exposure to OPEC+ scenario.

recommended_actions.description: Escalate to credit committee immediately. Request updated borrowing base certificate and review leverage covenant compliance given existing CRITICAL status.
Schedule covenant review within 10 business days. Obtain updated hedging schedule and assess borrowing base sensitivity to price reversal scenario.
Add energy input cost to Boeing's quarterly monitoring checklist. Watch for airline customer order deferrals that could affect Boeing's revenue recognition and debt service capacity.
